I‘ve implemented all of this in my pull request. I‘ll update it later so that it can be passed as parameter to np.save.Adding append functionality to np.save means that every time one wants to append something, one needs to read the old header, check it and modify it. This could be inefficient, but would be an easy-to-use solution.A class AppendArray that „knows“ the underlying array can append more efficiency, but can be provided by Numpy or some external library at any time in the future. I‘ll remove it from my pull request.A suggestion by Eric has been to add a „dirty“ flag to the header to indicate whether or not a write process was happening. What do you think about that? It would technically mean a modification of the format, but less major than adding a -1 to the shape and would probably not affect a lot of parsers as it would just be just an additional entry in a dict that would otherwise be ignored.Best from Berlin, MichaelPS sorry for my late reply and that I could not join the meeting on yesterday, was pretty busy with a trade fair this week.On 22. Sep 2022, at 17:22, Robert Kern ***@***.***> wrote:﻿
BTW, for the extra padding, just adding 64 bytes in _wrap_header() is going to cause some issues later in the appending process as the append-axis size goes larger. Applied naively, it would just mean that 64 bytes will be added to the larger header size, creating a new header that's larger than the old one. You'll have to handle that case explicitly.
A more advanced approach would be to check the current number of digits in the append-axis size's string representation, and only add enough padding to ensure that 20 digits (len(str(1<<64))) can fit. That will take a little refactoring of _write_array_header() and _wrap_header() since the padding is computed in the latter, but it only gets the string representation of the header. But quite doable.

Applied naively, it would just mean that 64 bytes will be added to the larger header size, creating a new header that's larger than the old one. You'll have to handle that case explicitly.
> 
> A more advanced approach would be to check the current number of digits in the append-axis size's string representation, and only add enough padding to ensure that 20 digits (`len(str(1<<64))`) can fit. That will take a little refactoring of `_write_array_header()` and `_wrap_header()` since the padding is computed in the latter, but it only gets the string representation of the header. But quite doable.

Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, In the first case, add spare space for growth","path":"numpy/lib/format.py","position":null,"original_position":15,"commit_id":"a7bde7fabed42db67bfd8dc6c247dd9bf596737b","original_commit_id":"f22a6ca189716a558176cc8bcbe7797f687e1ee5","user":{"login":"xor2k","id":3997849,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5OTc4NDk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/3997849?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k","html_url":"https://github.com/xor2k","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Just that I understand correctly: fixing `test_large_header()` implies that `_write_array_header()` can assume that the array is _correct enough_ which implies it must do the same sanity checks e.g. as `_read_array_header()`, which would then imply that `shape` and `fortran_order` is set (otherwise it would not make sense to modify `test_large_header()` and `_write_array_header()` in the first place).
Enabling sanity checks for `_write_array_header()` would need the tests `test_large_header()`, `test_bad_header()` and `test_metadata_dtype()` to be modified as well. While the first one is straight forward, the second one a little ugly and limits the readability of the test case slightly and could make the test case useless in case of future format modifications, the third one is a little challenging because `_write_array_header()` cannot produce certain kinds of invalid arrays anymore.
I've pushed with the `test_metadata_dtype()` failing.
I see three options to resolve the situation:
1. Revert to the old `_write_array_header()` variant that only applies spare space if the header dict is nice enough
2. Fix `test_metadata_dtype()` in a way that assumes that if a test fails, either writing or reading an array can fail as they share a portion of their code. However, if this is not the case in the future anymore or there is an issue in `np.save()` this could render the test useless. 
3. Introduce a context from `contextlib` for `_write_array_header()` to indicate that sanity checks should be skipped. However, I don't know if this is a thing in Numpy and how to properly implement it.
4. Introduce a boolean flag that would allow to write invalid Numpy headers. Probably 3 is less ugly.
What do you Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Michael","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/20321/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/20321/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/1257158009","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/20321#issuecomment-1257158009","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/20321","id":1257158009,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP85K7rV5","user":{"login":"xor2k","id":3997849,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5OTc4NDk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/3997849?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k","html_url":"https://github.com/xor2k","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-09-25T09:49:44Z","updated_at":"2022-09-25T09:49:44Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Help!!! Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Example: adding 64 bytes of spare space every time might lead to larger headers than before if one tries to append to the array. So on append, one either needs to check how large the array head used to be and add spare space accordingly with some if-clause _or_ think about what the minimum and maximum size of the array header actually is. The minimum size is assumed with just a single character in the decimal representation of the shape, e.g. `(1, 5, 5)` for C order. The maximum size is some 20 digit stuff like `(12345678901234567890, 5, 5)`. Therefore, one needs to take the digit count of the growth axis into account, like when it's `(1, 5, 5)` add 20-1=19 bytes of spare space elsewhere (e.g. at the end of the header). For `(12345678901234567890, 5, 5)` it's 20-20=0 bytes. Therefore, the array header size stays constant at all times (no matter how small or large the array is in the growth Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Actually, I had some extra thoughts about this: what if Numpy (or some other language using the .npy format), today or at some point in the future, supports/will support sub-byte datatypes like `int4`, `int2`, or `int1`? In Deep Learning, `int4` already is a thing. Let's construct something like `arr = np.array(range(17), dtype='int1')`. Then, since pointers still operate bytewise, `arr[15]=arr[1*8+7]` would be some 'resolve byte pointer and mask the return value'-operation (which could, depending on the arithmetic done on the array, be accelerated by the compiler and replaced by some higher level instructions, adding two `int1` is basically just an xor). However, the index could then become larger than `2**64-1`, actually up to `8*2**64-1`. I mean, does not make a big difference, but I'll give it 21 bytes with the next Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

Anyway, let's try this & best from Berlin, Dear all, I've added this pull request to add NpyAppendArray and have written to the mailing-list, see

https://mail.python.org/archives/list/numpy-discussion@python.org/thread/57TRDY3ZVMX3DYFHHPQOGWKDVFALSCPQ/

For a summary what NpyAppendArray is, compare

https://github.com/xor2k/npy-append-array

What I have done so far:
1. Added NpyAppendArray to lib.format
2. Added a test case to test_format.py
3. Verified that the test case is actually used by running np.lib.test(verbose=3)
4. Send an email to the mailing list (see above)

If you like the concept of NpyAppendArray, I will dedicate enough time to meet all numpy quality standards (which I have probably not met yet) and otherwise, if there is not so much interest, I'm prepared that this pull request may be deleted ;)

**Marking as draft, but I do not expect major changes, so this can get a first round of review, I think.** There is a small list of TODOs: * [ ] The current additional tests from npreadtext are missing here (they should be rewritten in terms of loadtxt probably). * [ ] Additional tests should be added (see also npreadtext issue tracker; one reason for the PR, it makes checking code-coverage more convenient.) * [ ] Smaller changes (e.g. I think I want to remove passing `usecols` as a numpy array) The [issues from `nreadtext`](https://github.com/BIDS-numpy/npreadtext/issues) still apply, and we should review some of these or make a call on them. The issue tracker there may also be a good way to open other smaller things to fix-up (to reduce noise here). --- As a general overview, the parser has a tokenizer, that works with unicode, but supports the Python modes UCS4, UCS2, and UCS1. It parses everything into smaller UCS4 (unicode) strings, this copies the data – in theory not always necessary, but convenient – although future optimization could be done. After this step, we have dtype (especially for integers) specific converter functions which directly convert the UCS4 string into the final result. If necessary, we fall back to `PyArray_Pack`. (Since we do not discover the result `dtype`, strings have to offload some work to Python currently.) In general, we read either line-by-line from Python iterables, or in chunks if reading from files. The new features are currently fairly limited (e.g. I removed the custom float parser to support using `d` instead of `e`), and is not fully CVS parser featured (but this can easily be achieved now!). One particular important changes is the current decision to remove hex float parsing and limit integer parsing to integers (we used to fall back to floats). The custom parsers also mean that we do not support numbers with an underscore `100_000` right now. Our current idea was that we should improve the error message a bit more, but generally advise users to use `converters=int` in this case. Please check the `npreadtext` issue tracker for issues/changes: https://github.com/BIDS-numpy/npreadtext/issues EDIT: One big additional feature (which I have not made public here, but is as easy as adding the kwarg), is that the parser does support quoting (with excel double-quote escaping).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/20580/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":1,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/20580/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/1008291333","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/20580#issuecomment-1008291333","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/20580","id":1008291333,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP848GU4F","user":{"login":"xor2k","id":3997849,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5OTc4NDk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/3997849?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k","html_url":"https://github.com/xor2k","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-01-09T12:47:17Z","updated_at":"2022-01-09T12:47:17Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I can't resist to comment here, so sorry in advance ;) Long time ago I have written my own CSV parser. It had support for quotes, I think that should be pretty much standard. CSV parsers are just inherently complex and need to handle legacy, since this is what CSV is about to a large amount ("I want to read that old file from this legacy system XYZ here"). I totally agree with @seberg that it is about the structure of the code rather than the language it is written in (C or Python) to keep this complexity as low as possible and a CSV parser maintainable. Maintainability and reliability should be more important than speed anyway, since there is not so much speed to be gained, as even GPUs can only accelerate by a factor of 2-3x, compare https://medium.com/rapids-ai/reading-larger-than-memory-csvs-with-rapids-and-dask-e6e27dfa6c0f Having CSV in a workflow is frustrating. It is always slow and when dealing with floats (so probably most of the time), it is a reason for precision loss. In my workflows, I typically convert CSV to HDF or .npy first and create intermediate, binary cache files if needed to have short cycle times ("save .py, automated run (e.g. with entr), see results" should take only a few seconds). However, one nice thing about CSV is that it can be easily appended to, just by writing to the end of the file and at this point, I'd like to advertise my pull request a little more: https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/20321 This would make .npy appendable in the same way CSV can be appended to: just write some more data to the end of the file, no need to update headers. **Marking as draft, but I do not expect major changes, so this can get a first round of review, I think.** There is a small list of TODOs: * [ ] The current additional tests from npreadtext are missing here (they should be rewritten in terms of loadtxt probably). * [ ] Additional tests should be added (see also npreadtext issue tracker; one reason for the PR, it makes checking code-coverage more convenient.) * [ ] Smaller changes (e.g. I think I want to remove passing `usecols` as a numpy array) The [issues from `nreadtext`](https://github.com/BIDS-numpy/npreadtext/issues) still apply, and we should review some of these or make a call on them. The issue tracker there may also be a good way to open other smaller things to fix-up (to reduce noise here). --- As a general overview, the parser has a tokenizer, that works with unicode, but supports the Python modes UCS4, UCS2, and UCS1. It parses everything into smaller UCS4 (unicode) strings, this copies the data – in theory not always necessary, but convenient – although future optimization could be done. After this step, we have dtype (especially for integers) specific converter functions which directly convert the UCS4 string into the final result. If necessary, we fall back to `PyArray_Pack`. (Since we do not discover the result `dtype`, strings have to offload some work to Python currently.) In general, we read either line-by-line from Python iterables, or in chunks if reading from files. The new features are currently fairly limited (e.g. I removed the custom float parser to support using `d` instead of `e`), and is not fully CVS parser featured (but this can easily be achieved now!). One particular important changes is the current decision to remove hex float parsing and limit integer parsing to integers (we used to fall back to floats). The custom parsers also mean that we do not support numbers with an underscore `100_000` right now. Our current idea was that we should improve the error message a bit more, but generally advise users to use `converters=int` in this case. Please check the `npreadtext` issue tracker for issues/changes: https://github.com/BIDS-numpy/npreadtext/issues EDIT: One big additional feature (which I have not made public here, but is as easy as adding the kwarg), is that the parser does support quoting (with excel double-quote escaping).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/20580/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":1,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/20580/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/1008292230","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/20580#issuecomment-1008292230","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/20580","id":1008292230,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP848GVGG","user":{"login":"xor2k","id":3997849,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5OTc4NDk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/3997849?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k","html_url":"https://github.com/xor2k","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/xor2k/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-01-09T12:53:20Z","updated_at":"2022-01-09T12:53:20Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"And I'd also like to add that most CSV parsers I've encountered (C, C++, JavaScript, Java) are simply broken and unmaintained.
